evals.name: breaking-news-all-platforms
evals.prompt: Hey, Anthropic just released Claude 5 with native computer use. Post about it on X, LinkedIn, Threads, Discord, and Telegram. Here's the blog post link: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-5
evals.expected_output: Should read product context if available, think strategically about hook and angle, check last 10 posts for duplicates, generate 3 image options with correct aspect ratios per platform (16:9 for X/Discord/Telegram, 1.91:1 for LinkedIn, 1:1 for Threads), create a proof screenshot for X and Threads (e.g. screenshot of the announcement page or key metrics), present all drafts + images for approval before posting, adapt copy per platform tone, use xurl for X if available, discord skill for Discord, Bot API for Telegram, save copy files, and log all URLs after posting.

assertions.text: Checks recent posts on target platforms before drafting to avoid duplicate content
Generates 3 image options (A/B/C) using Nano Banana Pro and presents them for user selection
Uses correct aspect ratios per platform: 16:9 for X/Discord/Telegram, 1.91:1 for LinkedIn, 1:1 for Threads
Presents all content drafts and images to user and explicitly waits for approval before posting anything
Adapts copy for each platform (punchy for X, professional for LinkedIn, casual for Threads, chat-style for Discord)
Attempts to use xurl for X, discord skill for Discord, and Telegram Bot API rather than browser automation
For X/Twitter and Threads, attempts to use or create a proof screenshot (announcement page, key metrics, evidence) as the first image rather than generic AI art

evals.name: instagram-tiktok-carousel
evals.prompt: Create an Instagram carousel about the top 5 AI coding tools in 2026. Also post it to TikTok as a video. Make the slides really eye-catching with Nano Banana Pro.
evals.expected_output: Should generate 5 carousel slides, each with 3 options (15 images total) at 1:1 square for Instagram. Present the grid for mix-and-match selection. After approval, create 9:16 vertical video for TikTok using Veo 3.1 AI video generation or ffmpeg slideshow conversion as fallback. Wait for approval before uploading to either platform.

assertions.text: Creates 5 carousel slides (hook, 3 content slides, CTA)
Generates 3 image options per slide (15 images total) and presents them for selection
Instagram images are generated at 1:1 square aspect ratio (1080x1080)
Creates 9:16 vertical video for TikTok using Veo 3.1 AI video generation or ffmpeg slideshow conversion as fallback
Shows all slides/video to user and waits for explicit approval before uploading to Instagram or TikTok

evals.name: daily-engagement-session
evals.prompt: Go engage on X and Reddit today. The topic is AI agent frameworks. Find some good threads and leave thoughtful replies. Also check LinkedIn for anything worth commenting on.
evals.expected_output: Should find 10 threads per platform (30 total across X, Reddit, LinkedIn), draft all replies as batches per platform, present for approval, respect anti-spam limits (especially Reddit spacing), use short 1-3 sentence replies without em dashes, and log all reply URLs.

assertions.text: Finds approximately 10 active threads per platform (X, Reddit, LinkedIn)
Presents all drafted replies as a batch per platform and waits for approval before posting any
Respects Reddit anti-spam rules: spaces comments apart, max 3 per thread per hour, max 5 total in 30 min
Replies are 1-3 sentences, add genuine value, no em dashes, sound human
Logs all reply URLs to a daily engagement log file after posting

evals.name: scheduled-posting-optimal-time
evals.prompt: I have a post about our new AI-powered code review tool launching next week. I want to post it on LinkedIn and X, but schedule it for the absolute best time based on how my past posts have performed. Also post to Threads when ready. Can you figure out the best time?
evals.expected_output: Should analyze past post engagement data (read 20-30 recent posts, identify top performers and their posting times), recommend a specific datetime with reasoning, use native scheduling on LinkedIn and X, note that Threads doesn't support scheduling, generate images, and get approval before scheduling.

assertions.text: Reads recent post history (20-30 posts) on target platforms to analyze engagement patterns and identify best-performing posting times
Recommends a specific date and time with reasoning based on analytics data, not just generic best practices
Uses native scheduling features on LinkedIn and X rather than posting immediately
Correctly identifies that Threads does not support native scheduling and proposes an alternative (post in real-time or set a reminder)
Presents the content, images, and proposed schedule time to user and waits for approval before scheduling

evals.name: topic-to-all-platforms
evals.prompt: Post about how AI coding agents are changing the way startups ship software. I have a blog post about this at dev.to. Make it punchy and opinionated.
evals.expected_output: Should read product-marketing-context.md if available, think strategically about hook and angle, write platform-adapted copy for all active platforms, generate images with Nano Banana Pro at correct aspect ratios per platform, create proof screenshot for X and Threads, save copy files to generated-assets/copy/, present everything with full absolute paths, use automated posting tools per priority order or Chrome-open as fallback.

assertions.text: Attempts to read product-marketing-context.md or asks user for brand context before writing copy
Explicitly considers hook formula, psychological angle, or content pillar before writing copy
Writes different copy for each platform (LinkedIn professional, Twitter punchy, Instagram visual-first) rather than copy-pasting
Saves platform-specific copy to individual .txt files in generated-assets/copy/
Uses Nano Banana Pro skill to generate images with correct aspect ratios per platform (16:9 for LinkedIn/Twitter, 1:1 for Instagram, 9:16 for TikTok/Stories)
Presents generated image paths as full absolute paths (not relative) and specifies which platform each image is for
Uses automated posting tools (xurl, Puppeteer, API) per the tool priority order, or opens Chrome with configured profile as a fallback for manual posting
Presents all content for review and waits for approval before posting or opening browser
For X/Twitter and Threads, attempts to use or create a proof screenshot (terminal output, metrics, evidence) as the first image rather than generic AI art

evals.name: product-launch-post
evals.prompt: We just hit 20,000 monthly npm downloads. Create a celebratory post for LinkedIn and Twitter. Keep it authentic, not braggy.
evals.expected_output: Should read product context for brand voice, craft a milestone-as-lesson angle (not just bragging), adapt for LinkedIn (professional depth) vs Twitter (punchy), generate images, present with full paths, guide the user through posting.

assertions.text: Adapts tone to match brand voice from product context (or asks for brand voice if no context file)
Frames the milestone as a lesson learned or behind-the-scenes story rather than pure self-congratulation
LinkedIn version is longer and more reflective; Twitter version is concise and punchy
Generates at least one image per platform using Nano Banana Pro with correct dimensions
Provides per-platform posting instructions with full paths to copy files and images

evals.name: no-context-file-graceful
evals.prompt: Post about the importance of code review in AI-generated code. Post to LinkedIn, Twitter, and Instagram.
evals.expected_output: Should attempt to find product-marketing-context.md, gracefully handle its absence (ask user for brief brand context or proceed with generic professional tone), still generate platform-adapted copy and images.

assertions.text: Attempts to read product-marketing-context.md before writing
If no context file found, either asks user for brand info or proceeds with sensible defaults rather than failing
Produces platform-adapted copy for all three platforms regardless of context file presence
Generates images (or attempts to) even without a context file

evals.name: repurpose-article
evals.prompt: I just published an article on dev.to called 'Why Your AI Coding Agent Gets Exponentially More Expensive.' Turn it into social posts for all platforms.
evals.expected_output: Should check published articles list in product context, extract key insights from the article, create platform-adapted posts that deliver standalone value (not just 'read my article'), generate images, and include the article link in first comments (not post body for LinkedIn).

assertions.text: Extracts 2-3 key insights or data points from the article to use as standalone social content
Posts deliver value on their own rather than being empty teasers like 'check out my new article'
Places the article link in first comment for LinkedIn (not post body), and appropriately for other platforms
Creates distinct copy for each active platform, not identical text
